human_verification.method: Manual review against issue #104, repository templates, authority-source requirements, and the validated delivery contract.

specialist_owned_sections: narrow applied-AI specialty boundary and rejection map
solution-pattern, guardrail, and escalation guidance specific to applied AI workflows
shared meta-agent bootstrap metadata and request/response contracts
fallback behavior for stale, unavailable, or conflicting delegated outputs
market_ready_claim_support: Complete package exists at the issue-specified catalog path.
Authority sources are real public sources with review dates, refresh intervals, and decay policy.
Evaluation results meet the 0.90 minimum pass-rate threshold with no unreviewed regressions.
Deployment and marketing artifacts document runtime, isolation, rollback, monitoring, buyer profile, subscription model, proof points, and go-to-market gaps.

specialist_owned_evidence: The manifest keeps the boundary narrowly focused on applied-AI solution design, guardrails, evaluation, and troubleshooting rather than product strategy, MLOps, or broad engineering.
Authority-source coverage spans official Microsoft, AWS, Google Cloud, Databricks, MLflow, and NIST guidance relevant to applied-AI solution delivery.
Issue-required shared meta-agent delegation and safe local fallback contracts are explicitly documented in manifest.yaml.
delegated_meta_agent_evidence: Best-practice corpus expansion is delegated to #225 with a safe local-baseline fallback.
Scenario normalization is delegated to #302 while retaining issue-local scenario coverage and acceptance thresholds.
Readiness, deployment, commercialization, and source-policy normalization are delegated to #303, #304, #305, and #306 without blocking safe package startup.
notes: validated claim is limited to repository packaging readiness and evidence completeness, not external revenue or customer adoption proof.
Human verification is recorded and no unreviewed regressions remain.
Adjacent product strategy, governance ownership, MLOps, knowledge-platform engineering, and unrelated infrastructure specialties are explicitly rejected.
